query: Africa CDC weekly brief Ebola DRC Ituri May 2026 latest report
answer:
## Research Report: Ebola Bundibugyo Outbreak in DRC Ituri Province — May 2026

### Overview

An active outbreak of **Bundibugyo ebolavirus disease (BVBD)** is underway in **Ituri Province, DRC**, first confirmed on **May 15, 2026**. WHO declared this a **Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC)** on May 16, 2026 — only the 9th PHEIC ever declared. Africa CDC called for urgent regional coordination on the same day.

---

### Latest Case Counts (Progression)

| Date | Suspected Cases | Confirmed Cases | Deaths | Source |
|------|----------------|----------------|--------|--------|
| **May 15** | 246 | 8 (lab-confirmed) | 80 | WHO DON602 |
| **May 19** | 536 | 34 | 131–134 | CARE; BBC; Al Jazeera |
| **May 20** | 600+ | 35 | 139+ | Project HOPE SitRep #1 |

- **Crude CFR:** ~25% (based on suspected cases)
- **Case surge:** Suspected cases more than doubled (246→536) in ~96 hours
- **Uganda:** 2 confirmed imported cases in Kampala; 1 death

---

### Affected Areas

- **Epicenter:** Mongbwalu Health Zone, Ituri Province (mining area, 3 health areas affected)
- **Rwampara Health Zone:** 6 health areas affected
- **Bunia Health Zone:** Provincial capital
- **Spread to North Kivu Province**, including **Goma** (densely populated transit hub)
- **International spread:** Uganda (Kampala), with alerts for Burundi, Rwanda, South Sudan

---

### Key Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| ~March 29 | First epidemic signs observed (Caritas) |
| April 24 | First suspected case (health worker) develops symptoms |
| May 5 | WHO alerted about unknown high-mortality illness in Mongbwalu |
| May 13 | DRC rapid response team investigates |
| May 15 | INRB confirms Bundibugyo virus; DRC declares 17th Ebola outbreak |
| May 16 | **WHO declares PHEIC**; **Africa CDC calls for regional coordination** |
| May 18 | American doctor tests positive; U.S. CDC imposes 30-day travel restrictions |
| May 19–20 | Deaths surpass 130; suspected cases exceed 500 |

---

### Vaccines & Therapeutics

- **No approved vaccine or treatment exists for Bundibugyo ebolavirus** — existing Ervebo/rVSV-ZEBOV vaccines target only the Zaire strain
- WHO considering experimental vaccines (as of May 19)
- PCR diagnostic cartridges for Bundibugyo virus in short supply

---

### Africa CDC Specific Information

The **Africa CDC weekly disease outbreak brief** landing page (`https://africacdc.org/weekly-disease-outbreaks-and-other-emergencies/`) could not be directly fetched due to tool limitations. However:
- Africa CDC issued a call for **urgent regional coordination on May 16, 2026**
- Africa CDC's outbreak-specific page: `https://africacdc.org/ebola-bundibugyo-drc-uganda-2026`
- The weekly brief would be expected to reflect the WHO SitRep 01 data (as of May 18) showing 536+ suspected cases and 131+ deaths

---

### Response Measures

| Organization | Action |
|-------------|--------|
| **WHO** | 11.5 tonnes of medical supplies; 35+ experts deployed; PHEIC declared |
| **Africa CDC** | Urgent regional coordination call (May 16) |
| **IFRC** | CHF 29M (~USD 34M) emergency appeal; 200 volunteers deployed |
| **MSF** | Medical/logistics teams mobilizing |
| **CARE** | Targeting 500K–1M people |
| **U.S. CDC** | 30-day travel restrictions from DRC, Uganda, South Sudan |

---

### Contributing Severity Factors

- ~3 weeks of undetected transmission before WHO alert
- U.S. funding cuts (March 2025) reduced IRC surveillance capacity in the epicenter
- Ongoing armed conflict and insecurity in eastern DRC
- Highly mobile populations (mining, cross-border trade)
- Early healthcare worker infections/deaths
- Critical PPE shortages
- Community resistance to public health measures

sources.snippet: U.S. funding cuts from March 2025 reduced IRC programming in Ituri from 5 to 2 health areas, weakening surveillance in the outbreak epicenter.

key_facts: An active Bundibugyo ebolavirus disease outbreak is underway in Ituri Province, DRC, confirmed on May 15, 2026 by INRB laboratory testing (8 of 13 samples positive).
WHO declared this a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) on May 16, 2026 — the 9th PHEIC in history.
Africa CDC called for urgent regional coordination on May 16, 2026; their outbreak page is at https://africacdc.org/ebola-bundibugyo-drc-uganda-2026.
As of May 20, 2026: 600+ suspected cases, 35 confirmed cases, and 139+ deaths (Project HOPE SitRep #1).
Crude case fatality rate is approximately 25% based on suspected cases.
The epicenter is Mongbwalu Health Zone in Ituri Province; spread has reached Rwampara, Bunia (Ituri), North Kivu (including Goma), and Uganda (2 imported cases in Kampala, 1 death).
No approved vaccine or treatment exists for Bundibugyo ebolavirus — existing Ervebo vaccine targets only Zaire strain.
Suspected cases more than doubled from 246 to 536 within approximately 96 hours (May 15–19).

The Africa CDC weekly disease outbreak brief page (https://africacdc.org/weekly-disease-outbreaks-and-other-emergencies/) was not directly retrievable; the most detailed epidemiological data comes from WHO DON602 and WHO SitRep 01.
Approximately 3 weeks of undetected transmission occurred before WHO was alerted on May 5, with the first suspected case dating to around April 24.
U.S. CDC imposed 30-day travel restrictions on DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan as of May 18, 2026.

data_points.metric: Crude case fatality rate (suspected case basis)
